metadata.description: Satya Promise Tracker — key promises made by Indian political leaders, linked to news evidence

promises.promise: Double farmer income by 2022
promises.source_description: PM Modi's address at farmers rally, Bareilly
promises.gemma_reasoning: The promise was made in 2014, and the provided evidence is from 2026.

evidence_articles.title: Modi government promised to double farm income by 2022 but it has only come down: Congress
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article reports on criticisms from the Congress party regarding the Modi government's unfulfilled 2016 promise to double farmers' income by 2022, alleging that real farm incomes have instead decreased.
evidence_articles.content: The Congress on Thursday claimed that the Modi government had promised to double the income of farmers by 2022 but instead their income had decreased, and accused the BJP of 'betraying' the farmers.

promises.notes: Multiple government reports and farmer groups confirmed income did not double by 2022. Inflation-adjusted farm income actually declined in several states.

promises.promise: Create 2 crore jobs every year
promises.gemma_reasoning: The promise is broken because the government has not created 2 crore jobs annually.

evidence_articles.title: PM Modi promised two crore jobs, but one crore people lost employment: Rahul Gandhi
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article details a youth rally where Rahul Gandhi targeted Prime Minister Modi on his promise to create two crore jobs annually, claiming that instead one crore jobs were lost due to economic policies.
evidence_articles.content: Addressing the 'Yuva Aakrosh Rally' in Jaipur, Congress leader Rahul Gandhi said while the Prime Minister had promised to provide two crore jobs to the youth every year, his government's policies had instead destroyed one crore jobs.

promises.notes: CMIE data consistently showed unemployment rising post-2016. India added far fewer formal jobs than promised annually.

promises.promise: Bring back black money from Swiss banks and deposit Rs 15 lakh in every Indian's account
promises.gemma_reasoning: The promise is not mentioned in the provided articles.

evidence_articles.title: FM defends Modi's 'Rs 15L for all' claim
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article covers Finance Minister Arun Jaitley defending PM Modi's 2014 campaign statement in the Sabha, explaining that the Rs 15 lakh figure was merely an illustrative calculation of the volume of stashed black money rather than a literal promise.
evidence_articles.content: Finance Minister Arun Jaitley on Wednesday defended Prime Minister Narendra Modi's election campaign statement that if all black money stashed abroad were brought back it could amount to Rs 15 lakh for each citizen. Jaitley told Rajya Sabha that the PM's remark was an 'illustrative statement' based on various versions of estimates of black money.

promises.notes: No black money repatriation happened. Rs 15 lakh was later called a 'jumla' by BJP president Amit Shah.

promises.promise: Build 2 crore pucca houses for the poor by 2022 (PM Awas Yojana)
promises.gemma_reasoning: The promise was made in 2014, and the deadline passed in 2022.

evidence_articles.title: 305 cities and towns identified for building homes under 'Housing for All by 2022' mission
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article covers the initial implementation phase of the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana, identifying 305 cities across nine states to kickstart the government's ambitious goal of constructing 2 crore affordable homes for the urban poor by 2022.
evidence_articles.content: Under the 'Housing for All by 2022' mission, the government has identified 305 cities and towns across nine states to start building houses for the urban poor. The housing ministry has also finalised reforms that need to be implemented by state governments to facilitate the construction of 2 crore houses.

promises.notes: Target missed in 2022, deadline extended. Partial completion achieved but far short of original target.

promises.promise: Make India a $5 trillion economy by 2024-25
promises.source_description: Budget session speech and multiple addresses
promises.gemma_reasoning: The promise is broken because the provided evidence focuses on energy prices and economic policies, not the economic growth target.

evidence_articles.title: India's delayed $5-trillion dream: What IMF's new timeline means for your wallet
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article discusses the shifting timeline for India to reach a $5 trillion economy, highlighting that the IMF's revised projections push the milestone to 2028-29, delayed from the government's initial target of 2024-25, primarily due to exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence_articles.content: According to the IMF's latest World Economic Outlook database, India is now expected to become a $5-trillion economy by 2028-29, delayed by four years from the government's initial target of 2024-25. The delay in achieving the target in dollar terms is primarily due to the depreciation of the rupee against the US dollar.

promises.notes: India's GDP remains around $3.5 trillion. Target missed significantly. COVID cited as reason.

promises.promise: End corruption completely — 'Na khaunga, na khane dunga'
promises.source_description: BJP campaign slogan and multiple rallies
promises.gemma_reasoning: Ongoing investigations suggest corruption persists.

evidence_articles.title: ‘Na khaunga na khane doonga’’ a complete hoax: Congress’ dig at PM on Arunachal CM’s kin matter in SC - The Hindu
evidence_articles.rephrased: The **Supreme Court** directed the **CBI** to investigate contracts awarded to firms allegedly owned by family members of Arunachal Pradesh Chief Minister Pema Khandu.  **Congress** general secretary
evidence_articles.content: The Congress on Monday (April 6, 2026) took a swipe at Prime Minister Narendra Modi after the Supreme Court directed the CBI to launch a preliminary inquiry into contracts allegedly awarded to Arunachal Pradesh Chief Minister Pema Khandu's family members, saying 'na khaunga na khane doonga' has been a "complete hoax" which is unravelling now. The Opposition party said this is just the beginning and a number of other BJP CMs are in the same category, "including at least one soon-to-be former CM from the Northeast". The Congress' attack came after the Supreme Court directed the CBI to register a preliminary enquiry within two weeks into the award of contracts for public works in Arunachal Pradesh to firms allegedly owned by family members of the chief minister. A bench headed by Justice Vikram Nath said the enquiry and consequential investigation, if any, shall cover the award and execution of public works, contracts and work orders in the state for the period from January 1, 2015 to December 31, 2025. Reacting to the development, Congress general secretary in-charge communications Jairam Ramesh said, "The Supreme Court has just directed the CBI to launch a preliminary inquiry into contracts allegedly awarded to family members of the Arunachal Pradesh CM." "This is just the beginning. A number of other BJP CMs are in the same category - including at least one soon-to-be former CM from the Northeast. 'Na khaunga na khane doonga' has been a complete hoax -- one that is unravelling now," Mr. Ramesh said on X. The Opposition has often referred to PM Modi's pledge to wipe out corruption — "Na khaunga na khane doonga" — and attacked him, alleging that it was mere rhetoric. The Supreme Court bench has directed the CBI to file a status report before it in the matter within 16 weeks. "The CBI shall register a PE within two weeks from the date of the judgement and will proceed in accordance with law," Justice Nath said while pronouncing the order. The top court had on February 17 reserved its order in the matter. During the hearing in the matter, the Supreme Court was told that government contracts and work orders worth around ₹1,270 crore were awarded in the last 10 years in Arunachal Pradesh to four firms related to the family members of Mr. Khandu. Published - April 06, 2026 03:22 pm IST

promises.notes: Multiple electoral bond scam revelations, Adani controversy, and multiple BJP ministers facing corruption allegations.

promises.promise: Electoral bonds will bring transparency to political funding
promises.gemma_reasoning: The evidence provided does not support the claim that electoral bonds bring transparency to political funding.

evidence_articles.title: Electoral bonds: PM Modi rejects corruption charge, says scheme was aimed at curbing black money
evidence_articles.rephrased: In an interview, Prime Minister Modi defended the electoral bonds scheme, arguing that it brought transparency by creating a clear money trail for political funding and aimed to eliminate black money in election campaigns.
evidence_articles.content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Monday rejected allegations of corruption over the electoral bonds scheme, saying the initiative was aimed at curbing black money in elections and bringing transparency. He said that prior to electoral bonds, there was no way of knowing where political parties got their funds from.

promises.notes: Supreme Court struck down electoral bonds as unconstitutional in Feb 2024. SBI data showed BJP received largest share of anonymous donations.

promises.promise: Har ghar nal se jal — piped water to every household by 2024
promises.source_description: Independence Day address, Jal Jeevan Mission launch
promises.gemma_reasoning: The promise is a long-term goal with no specific timeline provided.

evidence_articles.title: Centre's potable water mission may miss 2024 target
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article reports that the Union government's Jal Jeevan Mission, which aims to provide piped drinking water to every rural household by 2024, is likely to miss its target due to disruptions like the COVID-19 pandemic and materials shortages.
evidence_articles.content: The Union government’s ambitious initiative to provide tap water connections to all rural households by 2024 is likely to miss its target. The Union Jal Shakti Ministry reported that the COVID-19 pandemic, along with subsequent disruptions like the Russia-Ukraine war, has severely delayed the progress of the Jal Jeevan Mission.

promises.notes: Government claims high coverage but ground reports show many connections non-functional or irregular supply.

promises.promise: Uniform Civil Code implementation
promises.gemma_reasoning: Ongoing discussions and consultations regarding UCC implementation.

evidence_articles.title: Bengal polls 2026: Modi, Mamata spar over UCC implementation - The Hindu
evidence_articles.rephrased: **Prime Minister Narendra Modi** and **West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee** engaged in a heated exchange over the implementation of the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) ahead of the upcoming state ele
evidence_articles.content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee on Saturday (April 11, 2026) targeted each other over the implementation of the Uniform Civil Code in the poll-bound State. “The commitment of the BJP is to implement the Uniform Civil Code. By bringing UCC in the State, we will end the politics of appeasement forever,” Mr. Modi said, addressing a gathering in the Muslim-dominated Jangipur area of Murshidabad district. In its election manifesto released on Friday (April 10, 2026), the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) had promised to implement the UCC in the State within six months of being voted to power. According to the last Census, Muslims make up about 27% of the West Bengal population, and the community had largely opposed The Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Marriage) Act, 2019, and The Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025. ‘Battle for Bengali identity’ The Prime Minister addressed three public meetings at Jangipur, Kushmundi, and Kalna on Saturday (April 11, 2026), repeatedly reiterating his claims about demographic change taking place in the State, and calling the elections a “battle to preserve West Bengal’s identity.” “West Bengal will no longer tolerate this game of appeasement and vote-bank politics. We will not allow Bengalis to become a minority in the State,” Mr. Modi said. Addressing the rally at Kushmundi, the PM also reiterated that refugees belonging to the Matua and Namasudra communities — Hindus from Bangladesh —would be granted citizenship under the Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA). “Those who have suffered for decades and taken refuge here will get justice. The Matua and Namasudra communities will get citizenship under the CAA,” he said. In his speeches, the Prime Minister raised the pitch about fear prevailing during the Trinamool Congress regime. “Bhoy out, Bharsa In, BJP ke vote din (Fear out, trust in, vote for BJP),” Mr. Modi said. ‘BJP trying to divide the State’ Ms. Banerjee, who also addressed multiple rallies during the day, strongly denounced the UCC. “They have spoken about UCC in the manifesto... I will vehemently oppose this. They are in majority today so they will pass the Bill. When they won’t be in majority tomorrow, we will revoke the Bill... Free and fair elections are not possible as long as they remain,” she said. The Chief Minister also claimed that the BJP wants to divide the State. “Elections are going on and in the middle of that, they are bringing the Delimitation Bill in Parliament. This was not even debated. The reason behind this is that they want to divide Bengal and conduct the [National Register of Citizens] NRC here,” she said. The Trinamool Congress chairperson accused the BJP of trying to get her candidature from Bhabanipur seat in south Kolkata cancelled by attempting to file two false cases against her. However, the bid was foiled by TMC workers and the public, she said. Hectic campaigning With less than two weeks left for the first phase of polling in the State on April 23, Saturday (April 11, 2026) turned out to be a day of hectic political campaigning. Union Home Minister Amit Shah also addressed rallies at Onda and Chhatna in Bankura district. Mr. Shah said that the BJP would end the TMC’s “syndicate raj” if voted to power, and would ensure justice for women facing atrocities. “Mamata Banerjee plays the victim card. At times, she wears a bandage on her leg or head. This time, even if you wear a bandage on your leg, head, or hand, the people of Bengal will not vote for you,” the Home Minister said, referring to Ms. Banerjee’s campaign in a wheelchair with a plaster on her leg following an injury while canvassing at Nandigram in 2021. Addressing a gathering at Rejinagar, Trinamool general secretary Abhishek Banerjee alleged that Congress leader Adhir Ranjan Chowdhury, Aam Janata Unnayan Party president Humayun Kabir, and Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Gyanesh Kumar are the three “agencies” working to undermine the people and strengthen the BJP in West Bengal’s Murshidabad district. Mr. Banerjee accused the CEC of “taking away voting rights” of the poor under the guise of logical discrepancies in the electoral rolls. Published - April 11, 2026 10:39 pm IST

evidence_articles.title: UCC implementation in Assam: Muslim organisations seek consultations with government - The Hindu
evidence_articles.rephrased: **Muslim religious and social organizations** in Assam have urged the **Assam government** to consult with them before implementing the Uniform Civil Code (UCC). The organizations, including factions

promises.notes: In three consecutive manifestos. Uttarakhand passed state UCC in 2024. National UCC not yet implemented.

promises.promise: One nation one election
promises.gemma_reasoning: The provided evidence focuses on political controversies and internal party dynamics, not on the implementation of 'One Nation One Election'.

evidence_articles.title: Simultaneous polls: Top 10 recommendations made by Ram Nath Kovind panel
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article outlines the key recommendations of the Ram Nath Kovind-led committee on 'One Nation, One Election,' which proposed a two-step transition to simultaneous polls for the Lok Sabha, state assemblies, and local bodies.
evidence_articles.content: A high-level committee on 'One Nation, One Election' headed by former President Ram Nath Kovind submitted its report to President Droupadi Murmu on Thursday, recommending a two-step approach to hold simultaneous elections for the Lok Sabha, State Legislative Assemblies and local bodies.

promises.notes: Bill introduced in Parliament in 2024. Joint Parliamentary Committee examining it. Not yet implemented.

promises.promise: Demonetization will end black money and terrorism funding
promises.source_description: PM address to nation on demonetization
promises.gemma_reasoning: The promise is contradicted by the evidence of rising inflation and concerns about the impact of demonetization.

evidence_articles.title: Demonetisation a 'well-considered' decision to combat black money, terror financing: Centre tells SC
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article covers the Union government filing an affidavit in the Supreme Court, defending the 2016 demonetization of high-value notes as a well-considered decision that successfully targeted tax evasion, counterfeit currency, black money, and terror funding.
evidence_articles.content: Defending its November 2016 decision to demonetise high-value currency notes, the Union government has told the Supreme Court that the step was a 'well-considered' decision and was part of a larger strategy to combat the menace of fake currency, terror financing, black money and tax evasion.

promises.notes: RBI reported 99.3% of demonetized notes returned. Black money holdings largely unaffected. Terrorism funding continued.

promises.promise: Bullet train between Mumbai and Ahmedabad by 2023
promises.source_description: Bullet train project launch with Japan PM
promises.gemma_reasoning: The promise is ongoing as the project is approved, but not yet completed.

evidence_articles.title: COVID snag in bullet train project, may fail to meet 2023 deadline
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article reports that the Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project is likely to miss its December 2023 deadline due to delays in land acquisition in Maharashtra and disruptions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence_articles.content: The ambitious Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project, which was scheduled to be completed by December 2023, is likely to miss its deadline due to delays in land acquisition, especially in Maharashtra, and the COVID-19 pandemic.

promises.notes: Land acquisition issues, cost overruns. New deadline pushed to 2027-2028. Less than 30% construction complete.

promises.promise: Remove Rohingyas from India completely
promises.gemma_reasoning: The provided text does not mention Amit Shah's promise to remove Rohingyas from India.

evidence_articles.title: Shah asks Delhi Police to take action against networks helping illegal Bangladeshis, Rohingyas
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article covers Union Home Minister Amit Shah's directive to the Delhi Police during a review meeting, ordering them to take strict action to identify and deport illegal Bangladeshi and Rohingya immigrants, calling their presence a national security issue.
evidence_articles.content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Friday directed the Delhi Police to take strict action against networks facilitating the entry and stay of illegal Bangladeshi and Rohingya immigrants. Mr. Shah, who chaired a high-level meeting to review the law and order situation in Delhi, said that the presence of 'illegal intruders' is a matter of national security and directed that they should be identified and deported.

promises.notes: Rohingya population continues to live in India. Deportations happen occasionally but not systematically.

promises.promise: Free and world class government schools in Delhi
promises.gemma_reasoning: No information provided about Arvind Kejriwal's promise regarding free and world-class government schools in Delhi.

evidence_articles.title: Atishi turns spotlight on 'world-class' govt. schools in Delhi; BJP dismisses her claims
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article covers Education Minister Atishi showcasing the Delhi government's work since 2015 to build over 22,000 new classrooms and upgrade infrastructure to world-class standards, while the BJP criticized the claims for ignoring high teacher vacancies and declining pass percentages.
evidence_articles.content: Delhi Education Minister Atishi on Friday claimed that the AAP government has built 22,711 new classrooms in the last nine years, transforming the state of education and providing world-class facilities to students. The BJP dismissed the claims as 'misleading', alleging that the government has failed to fill teacher vacancies and that many schools are functioning without principals.

promises.notes: Delhi government school results improved significantly. International recognition received. One of AAP's few kept promises.

promises.promise: Free electricity up to 200 units in Delhi
promises.gemma_reasoning: The provided text does not contain any information about the status of the free electricity promise.

evidence_articles.title: Kejriwal promises free 200 units electricity for tenants
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article covers Arvind Kejriwal's pre-election announcement promising to resolve execution flaws in the prepaid meter scheme so that tenants in Delhi can easily get free electricity up to 200 units and free water.
evidence_articles.content: AAP national convener Arvind Kejriwal on Saturday said that if re-elected, his government will resolve issues regarding the prepaid meter system so that all tenants in Delhi can easily avail themselves of free electricity up to 200 units and water up to 20,000 litres.

promises.notes: Implemented and sustained. Free electricity up to 200 units provided to Delhi households.

promises.promise: Clean Yamuna river by 2025
promises.gemma_reasoning: The provided articles do not mention anything about Arvind Kejriwal's promise to clean the Yamuna river.

evidence_articles.title: Delhi govt working on war footing to clean Yamuna by February 2025: Arvind Kejriwal
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article covers Arvind Kejriwal's announcement of a six-point action plan to clean the heavily polluted Yamuna river to bathing standards by February 2025, which includes increasing sewage treatment capacity and connecting all slums to the sewer network.
evidence_articles.content: Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal on Thursday announced a six-point action plan to clean the Yamuna river to 'bathing standards' by February 2025. He said the government is working on a war footing and he is personally monitoring the progress of the projects.

promises.notes: Yamuna remains one of India's most polluted rivers. Supreme Court repeatedly pulled up Delhi government over inaction.

promises.promise: Guarantee minimum income of Rs 72,000 per year to poorest families (NYAY scheme)
promises.gemma_reasoning: Rahul Gandhi has proposed a NYAY scheme, but it has not been implemented.

evidence_articles.title: Congress promises ₹72,000 per annum to poorest people
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article covers Rahul Gandhi's announcement of the NYAY minimum income guarantee scheme, which promises to directly transfer ₹72,000 per year to the bank accounts of India's poorest 20% families.
evidence_articles.content: Congress president Rahul Gandhi on Monday announced a minimum income guarantee scheme, Nyuntam Aay Yojana (NYAY), under which ₹72,000 a year will be transferred to the bank accounts of 20% of the poorest families in the country if his party is voted to power.

promises.notes: Congress lost 2019 election. NYAY scheme never implemented. Remains a campaign promise.

promises.promise: End mafia raj and crime completely in Uttar Pradesh
promises.source_description: CM oath taking speech and subsequent addresses
promises.gemma_reasoning: The provided articles do not mention anything about Yogi Adityanath's promise regarding mafia and crime.

evidence_articles.title: With zero tolerance for crime and criminals, U.P. has created sense of security for industry: Yogi Adityanath
evidence_articles.rephrased: The article covers Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath highlighting the state's zero-tolerance policy toward crime and criminals, claiming it has restored law and order and established a safe environment that attracts industrial investment.
evidence_articles.content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath on Tuesday said the State government's policy of 'zero tolerance' towards crime and criminals had restored law and order and created a sense of security, making it a preferred destination for industrial investments.

promises.notes: Bulldozer politics became signature. Crime stats show mixed results. Encounter killings rose significantly raising human rights concerns.

promises.promise: Ma Mati Manush — government for people of Bengal
promises.gemma_reasoning: The evidence shows ongoing work related to the promise.

evidence_articles.title: Names of people belonging to Matua, minority communities deleted from post-SIR voter rolls: Mamata - The Hindu
evidence_articles.rephrased: **Mamata Banerjee**, West Bengal Chief Minister, accused the **Union government** of targeting specific communities by deleting names from voter rolls during the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) proce
evidence_articles.content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee on Tuesday (April 7, 2026) alleged that names of people belonging to the Matua and minority communities were deleted from the post-SIR voter rolls in the State. Addressing a rally at Chakdaha in Nadia district, Ms. Banerjee said that the TMC will stand by those whose names were excluded from the voter rolls, following the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) exercise. Assembly elections April 7, 2026 LIVE: Campaigning ends in Kerala, Assam, Puducherry today According to the latest data by the Election Commission of India (ECI), nearly 91 lakh voters have been deleted from the electoral rolls in West Bengal after the SIR process. The poll panel is yet to announce the finally altered voter base for the state after the roll revision process. As per official data released on February 28, 63.66 lakh names, around 8.3% of the electorate, were deleted since the SIR process began in November last year, reducing the voter base from about 7.66 crore to just over 7.04 crore. Accusing the Centre of discrimination, Ms. Banerjee alleged that names were being removed from voter rolls by targeting specific communities. “Why this discrimination? You are excluding Matuas, Rajbanshis and minorities. Do you think that people do not understand this?” the CM said. She also claimed that in districts with significant minority population such as Murshidabad, Malda and North Dinajpur, names were “picked and removed like lice” from the electoral rolls. Ms. Banerjee asserted that following her intervention in the Supreme Court, around 32 lakh names out of nearly 60 lakh cases under ‘adjudication’ had been restored. “Those whose names are still missing should apply before the tribunal. The TMC will stand by you and provide legal support,” she said. Questioning the Centre’s stand on infiltration, Ms. Banerjee said the Union government has the responsibility of protecting the country’s borders, but it was blaming the West Bengal government for infiltration. Describing the upcoming Assembly polls as a battle to protect people’s identity and democratic rights, the TMC supremo urged voters to respond through the ballot. “This election is a fight to save your democracy, language and respect so that no one can ever call you a foreigner,” Ms. Banerjee said. At another poll rally in Bongaon, the CM asserted that the Trinamool Congress will win more seats than before, and form government for the fourth consecutive term. “The TMC will ultimately win and get more seats than before,” she said. The party had won 215 seats in the 2021 elections for the 294-member West Bengal assembly. “This election is to avenge the deletion of names from the voter list,” Ms. Banerjee said. The TMC chief claimed that names of 300 members of Missionaries of Charity, which was formed by Mother Teresa, have been deleted in the electoral rolls revision exercise, and also those of monks of the Ramakrishna Mission and Bharat Sevasram Sangha. Ms. Banerjee also said that several names have been excluded from the rolls in Murshidabad, Uttar Dinajpur, Malda, South 24 Parganas and Nadia districts, while reiterating that in Bhabanipur constituency, where she is contesting for re-election, 40,000 names have been struck off. “We will have to see how many names have been restored in the supplementary list... I will not allow detention camps to be set up (in Bengal). Constitutional rights must be given, and I will continue to fight for restoration of these,” she said. In a bid to woo the Matua community, who have a strong presence in several assembly constituencies in North 24 Parganas and Nadia district, Ms. Banerjee said that her government has initiated a lot of development works in those areas. The CM also accused the BJP of trying to divide Matua community votes. Attacking Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Ms. Banerjee claimed that he had lunch with leaders of Gulf countries and hugged them, with religion not becoming a barrier. “When you go to Gulf countries, you hug the Muslim rulers,” she said. Ms. Banerjee also claimed that cattle are exported from the country, but it does not become an issue as to whether that is right or wrong. “Their (BJP) religion is to divide votes, that is not Hinduism,” the TMC chief added. Published - April 07, 2026 02:08 pm IST

promises.notes: Bengal saw significant political violence. Syndicate raj and TMC worker dominance reported widely.

promises.promise: Viksit Bharat by 2047 — developed India by centenary of independence
promises.gemma_reasoning: The target year is 2047 and active work is mentioned.

evidence_articles.title: PM Modi assures more support to Telangana, asks CM Revanth to collaborate with him - The Hindu
evidence_articles.rephrased: **Prime Minister Narendra Modi** assured **Telangana Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy** that the **Central government** would continue to support its development, emphasizing the importance of state-le

promises.notes: Long term vision. Too early to assess. Used as campaign narrative in 2024 elections.
